text.title: OpenAPI Authoring Rules and CI Gates
text.summary: Mandatory rules for spec authoring and CI enforcement: $ref reuse, operationId, error schema, breaking-change gate, server-generated spec drift detection.

rule.statement: Run redocly lint and spectral lint --fail-severity=warn on every PR. Configure Spectral rules: require description on all operations, ban additionalProperties: true on response schemas, require at least one 2xx and one 4xx response per operation.
rule.rationale: Undescribed operations produce useless docs. additionalProperties: true breaks forward-compat in generated clients that use discriminated union types. Missing 4xx responses omit the error contract.

rule.statement: Gate every PR with oasdiff breaking comparing HEAD to main. Fail the build on any BREAK classification. A breaking change requires human sign-off; never let an agent merge a BREAK diff unattended.
rule.rationale: Breaking changes (path removal, required field addition, enum shrinkage) silently break existing clients. Automated detection prevents accidental breakage from merging.

rule.statement: For FastAPI / drf-spectacular / NestJS: commit the generated openapi.yaml snapshot to git and add a CI step that regenerates it and fails if the output differs from the committed version.
rule.rationale: Server-generated specs can drift from the committed snapshot when developers change models without regenerating. CI drift detection prevents the docs from lying about the API.

example.code: # Regenerate spec from running app fastapi run --export-openapi /tmp/current-openapi.yaml # Diff against committed version diff openapi.yaml /tmp/current-openapi.yaml || \ (echo "ERROR: openapi.yaml is stale. Run: fastapi run --export-openapi openapi.yaml" && exit 1)

rule.statement: Split specs using redocly split once the paths object exceeds 30 endpoints. Reassemble with redocly bundle for distribution. Large monolithic specs become unreadable diffs and slow linters.
rule.rationale: At 5k+ lines, linters slow, diffs are unreadable, and agents lose track of $ref graphs. Splitting into per-tag files restores locality without losing the single-bundle contract.
